data.title: Delete all delete-classified change-detection tests and clean up .test-index and KNOWN_FAILING
data.description:
## Testing Mode
GREEN

## Description
Read the triage classification results from story ca06-e969's ticket comments (the comment containing the delete/replace/keep classification table). For each test file classified as "delete":

1. Remove the test file from tests/skills/ or tests/agents/
2. Remove all .test-index entries that reference the deleted file (as test target, not source)
3. Remove any KNOWN_FAILING entries in tests/skills/run-python-tests.sh that reference deleted tests
4. Validate the test suite passes after all deletions

## TDD Requirement
TDD exemption: Criterion 2 (change-detector removal — removing files without altering observable behavior) and Criterion 3 (static config file updates to .test-index and KNOWN_FAILING with no branching logic). Green state verified by universal test criterion.

## File Impact
- tests/skills/*.py (delete-classified files — REMOVE)
- tests/agents/*.sh (delete-classified files — REMOVE)
- .test-index (EDIT — remove entries pointing to deleted test files)
- tests/skills/run-python-tests.sh (EDIT — remove stale KNOWN_FAILING entries)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] bash tests/run-all.sh passes (exit 0)
  Verify: cd $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel) && bash tests/run-all.sh
- [ ] ruff check passes (exit 0)
  Verify: cd $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel) && ruff check plugins/dso/scripts/*.py tests/**/*.py
- [ ] ruff format --check passes (exit 0)
  Verify: cd $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel) && ruff format --check plugins/dso/scripts/*.py tests/**/*.py
- [ ] After cleanup, no .test-index entries reference nonexistent test files
  Verify: cd $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel) && python3 -c "import os,re,sys;[sys.exit(1) for line in open('.test-index') if not line.strip().startswith('#') and ':' in line for t in line.split(':',1)[1].split(',') if (tc:=re.sub(r'\[.*?\]','',t).strip()) and not os.path.exists(tc)]"
- [ ] After cleanup, KNOWN_FAILING entries correspond to existing test functions
  Verify: cd $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel) && python3 -c "import re,subprocess,sys;c=open('tests/skills/run-python-tests.sh').read();m=re.search(r'KNOWN_FAILING=\((.*?)\)',c,re.DOTALL);[sys.exit(1) for e in re.findall(r'\"([^\"]+)\"',m.group(1)) if subprocess.run(['grep','-rq',e,'tests/'],capture_output=True).returncode!=0] if m else None"
